required_signals: counterpart actor ID; timestamp; speed; acceleration or braking state; optional ego-relative position for interaction context
units: timestamp ms; speed source units; acceleration source units; position source length units
sampling_rate_needed: approximately 10 Hz or finer; observed vehicle/world trajectory median 100 ms
time_alignment_need: counterpart state must align to ego/world timestamp within the same replay frame or nearest 100 ms frame
actor_attribution_need: requires a frozen counterpart identity rule using neutral actor IDs from simulation_trajectory.value.value[].id/originId and/or perception participant IDs
derivability_notes: World actor acceleration, speed, x/y, courseAngle, length, width, id, and originId are present in simulation_trajectory.log; hard-braking threshold is later W2 work and was not set here.
missingness_risk: low for simulation world actors in inspected rows: speed/acceleration/id/x/y 0 missing; counterpart identity rule not yet frozen.
gate_012_0_reason: Retain because actor-level speed/acceleration/time/ID channels exist at about 10 Hz; threshold and counterpart rule remain later gated work.

required_signals: ego or actor acceleration; speed; timestamp; actor ID
units: timestamp ms; acceleration source units; speed source units
sampling_rate_needed: approximately 10 Hz; observed median 100 ms
time_alignment_need: single-actor time series aligned by timestamp
actor_attribution_need: neutral ego/world actor IDs are sufficient
derivability_notes: Acceleration is directly recorded in ego vehicle_trajectory.log and world simulation_trajectory.log; alternate spelling acceleration/acceleration observed.
missingness_risk: low: world acceleration 0 missing in inspected rows; ego acceleration has 83 missing values for one spelling but alternate acceleration field covers most rows.
gate_012_0_reason: Retain because deceleration is directly recorded for ego and world actors; W2 must define threshold and spelling precedence.

required_signals: acceleration time series or speed time series; timestamp; actor ID
units: timestamp ms; acceleration source units; jerk derived as acceleration unit per second
sampling_rate_needed: 10 Hz minimum; observed trajectory cadence about 100 ms but per-actor smoothing needed
time_alignment_need: same actor consecutive frames on a normalized time grid
actor_attribution_need: neutral actor ID continuity across frames
derivability_notes: Jerk is not directly recorded but can be derived from acceleration deltas over timestamp in vehicle_trajectory.log or simulation_trajectory.log.
missingness_risk: moderate: derivative amplifies timestamp jitter and acceleration spelling/missingness; requires interpolation/smoothing rules.
gate_012_0_reason: Retain because acceleration and timestamp channels exist; derivative method must be frozen before extraction.

required_signals: ego/counterpart/non-counterpart trajectories; right-of-way or intended route; gap/proximity; speed/deceleration; human context for forcedness
units: timestamp ms; speed/acceleration source units; position source length units; route/lane units unavailable
sampling_rate_needed: 10 Hz kinematics plus contextual route/right-of-way evidence
time_alignment_need: multi-actor aligned trajectories around interaction onset/resolution
actor_attribution_need: counterpart identity and yielding actor identity must be frozen
derivability_notes: Slowdown/yield kinematics are observable, but no lane/route/right-of-way/map field was found; forcedness is a semantic interaction judgment.
missingness_risk: high: essential route/right-of-way signal unavailable; optional media lacks fps/time alignment metadata.
gate_012_0_reason: Demote because kinematics alone cannot establish forcedness or right-of-way without route/lane/context signals.

required_signals: yielding role over time; counterpart identity; right-of-way or expected priority; actor trajectories
units: timestamp ms; speed/position source units; route/lane/right-of-way unavailable
sampling_rate_needed: 10 Hz kinematics plus role/context timeline
time_alignment_need: multi-actor aligned time windows
actor_attribution_need: stable counterpart and yielding-role labels
derivability_notes: Stop/slowdown sequences can be derived, but role reversal requires semantic role/right-of-way interpretation not present in schema.
missingness_risk: high: no route/lane/right-of-way fields; role semantics unavailable.
gate_012_0_reason: Demote because no credible automatic signal path exists for role reversal semantics.

required_signals: speed time series; timestamp; actor ID; optional acceleration
units: timestamp ms; speed source units; acceleration source units
sampling_rate_needed: 10 Hz is sufficient for stop/go cycles after W2 duration and merge-gap rules
time_alignment_need: single-actor consecutive frames; multi-actor alignment optional for context
actor_attribution_need: neutral ego/world actor ID continuity
derivability_notes: Speed and timestamp exist for ego and world actors; stop-go cycles are derivable after threshold/duration rules are frozen.
missingness_risk: low for speed/time/id; threshold sensitivity and short gaps remain W2/W3 concerns.
gate_012_0_reason: Retain because speed/time/ID channels exist at about 10 Hz for ego and world actors.

required_signals: stop event; route/lane/right-of-way; obstacle and traffic-light context; human judgment of necessity
units: timestamp ms; speed source units; route/lane unavailable
sampling_rate_needed: 10 Hz kinematics plus contextual route/right-of-way signals
time_alignment_need: ego, other actors, obstacles, and traffic lights aligned in time
actor_attribution_need: stopped actor and potential cause actor/object
derivability_notes: A stop is derivable from speed; unnecessary status is not because lane/route/right-of-way and intent context are absent.
missingness_risk: high: essential necessity context unavailable; traffic lights exist but do not provide route priority alone.
gate_012_0_reason: Demote because automatic stop detection is possible but unnecessary-stop interpretation lacks route/context signals.

required_signals: multi-actor proximity; closing speed; deceleration/jerk; interaction context; severity progression
units: timestamp ms; speed/acceleration/position source units
sampling_rate_needed: 10 Hz multi-actor trajectories plus context
time_alignment_need: ego and relevant actors aligned by timestamp over an interaction window
actor_attribution_need: all involved neutral actor IDs and counterpart identity

missingness_risk: moderate-high: kinematics exist, but escalation semantics and thresholds are unfrozen; no route/lane.
gate_012_0_reason: Demote for now because no complete automatic definition of escalation is supported by direct schema fields.

missingness_risk: high: generic monitor status has null monitor objects in some rows and no semantic dictionary.
gate_012_0_reason: Remove from automatic extraction until an explicit intervention flag or authoritative status-code dictionary is supplied.
